question: Under the pinned endogenous migration-race model (migration-bearing PRs arrive Poisson lambda in {1, 4, 12} per day, develop W = 8 h from number-pick to push, validate V in {0.25, 2, 24} h, collision fix latency d = 0.5 h, horizon H = 2,000 h per run with the first 200 h discarded as warm-up, M = 40 seeded replications per cell-policy, random.Random(20260723), pinned loop order; policies by number-pick time — P0 picks at open and at each fix start (round-1 exposure W+V, later rounds d+V), P1 re-picks at every push (every round's exposure = V exactly), P3 assigns at the merge instant (exposure 0 — must measure exactly zero collisions, a built-in control); Arm A exact and seedless: P(N>=1) = p(w1), P(N>=2) = p(w1)*p(w2), E[N] = p(w1)*e^(lambda*w2) with p(w) = 1 - e^(-lambda*w); Arm S re-run in exogenous mode (external Poisson appends, one focal PR, M = 20,000 per cell-policy, seed 20260726) must agree with Arm A within 1.0 percentage point absolute on P(N>=1) and P(N>=2) in every covered cell or the run is invalid; reporting-only legs: sensitivity d in {0.25, 2} and W in {1, 24}, jitter leg seed 20260725, stability leg M = 8 seed 20260724, the per-cell endogenous/exogenous amplification ratio, latency inflation vs P3, and the #1279 anchor leg), what are R (renumbers per merged PR) and T (share of merged PRs with >= 2 renumbers) per endogenous (lambda, V) cell under P1 — and does the result land APPROVE / REJECT / NULL per the pre-registered rule, evaluated in that order?

source.idea_entry: ideas/superbot/migration-renumber-treadmill-residual-2026-07-13.md @ idea-engine main 8022a9d (proposal's own blob link @ e575260dc326e2dc5f49be33fc271d73a3ee16a5)
source.rotation_slot: inbox ORDER 004 rule 3 — FLEET-BACKLOGS rotation slot, round 2 (round 1: 019 fleet-backlogs / 018 venture / 020 game-mechanics / 017 unrelated); fully hermetic (the PROPOSAL 017/018/019/020 precedent): every fixture is a pinned constant committed with the sim, zero repo/network reads in the verdict session
source.harvest_anchor: superbot docs/ideas/migration-number-collision-guard-2026-06-22.md @ fd638e3 (byte-same at live HEAD 4522522, Option-1 checker answering 200) — the recorded incident: reaction-roles PR #1279, a held PR renumbered four times (085 -> 086 -> 088 -> 089) in one afternoon; the doc's open fork: Option 1 (shipped re-pick-at-push checker) vs Option 3 (assign-number-at-merge)

process.lifecycle: a PR opens at its Poisson arrival instant, develops for W hours (open -> push), then validates for V hours (push -> merge attempt); at the merge attempt, if the PR's held number has meanwhile been merged by another PR, that is a COLLISION: the PR renumbers (fix latency d hours), then re-validates (another V) and re-attempts; otherwise it merges and its number lands on main
process.number_arithmetic: main is a single shared append point: a pick takes max(main) + 1 at the pick instant; a successful merge appends the held number to main (main stays contiguous by construction: a merge attempt with held <= max(main) is a collision, so every successful merge lands exactly max+1 — checked as an invariant); collision test at attempt: held <= max(main)
process.policies: pick times only — P0: picks at open and at each fix START (round-1 exposure W+V, later rounds d+V; the fix-start pick happens at the collision-detection instant); P1: re-picks at every PUSH, i.e. at the start of every validation window (round-1 pick at open+W, after a fix at attempt+d; every round's exposure = V exactly — the shipped Option-1 checker's semantics); P3: assigns at the merge instant (exposure 0; the sim MUST measure exactly zero collisions — the built-in control)
process.event_order: continuous-time event queue; events processed in (time, PR id) ascending order (ties broken by PR id, per the pre-registration); a pick processed at the same instant as an earlier-processed merge sees the incremented main
process.horizon: arrivals over [0, H], H = 2,000 h; events after H are not processed; PRs unmerged at H are censored out of the merged population
process.warmup_population: metrics per (cell, policy) are computed on the merged-PR population after warm-up: PRs whose merge instant t > 200 h; each such PR contributes its FULL renumber count and FULL open->merge duration (even if parts predate warm-up); pooled over the M replications
process.metrics: R = total renumbers / merged PRs (exact Fraction on pooled integer counts); T = merged PRs with >= 2 renumbers / merged PRs (exact Fraction); reporting-only: mean and p95 open->merge duration and their inflation ratios vs the same cell's measured P3 leg, max simultaneous holders of one number (per (cell, policy) max over reps), and the endogenous/exogenous amplification ratio R_endo / E[N]_ArmA per cell for P0 and P1
process.draw_order_fixed_legs: per replication, the ONLY randomness is the Poisson arrival sequence: rng.expovariate(lambda_per_hour) gaps drawn until the cumulative arrival time exceeds H (the final overshoot draw is consumed, its arrival discarded); everything else is deterministic given the arrivals
process.loop_order: cells lexicographic (lambda ascending 1 < 4 < 12 per day, then V ascending 0.25 < 2 < 24 h), policy P0, P1, P3, replications sequential 0..M-1; ONE random.Random(seed) stream flows through the whole leg in this pinned order
process.exogenous_mode: the same engine re-run with appends = an external Poisson process at rate lambda and ONE focal PR (id 0, arrival at t = 0) under the policy on test; appends are modeled as instant-merge external PRs (P3 semantics, W = V = 0) at Poisson times drawn as expovariate gaps on [0, W+V+d+V] per focal PR (gaps drawn until cumulative exceeds that horizon; draws per focal = appends + 1); loop order: cells lexicographic, policy P0 then P1, focal PRs sequential 0..19,999, one Random(20260726) stream
process.p95: sorted pooled durations, index ceil(0.95*n) - 1 (the smallest value >= 95% of the sample)

constants.cells: 9 = 3 lambda x 3 V; cell key format lam%02d|V%05.2f (zero-padded so lexicographic string order == the pinned numeric order)

arm_a.definition: exact, seedless exogenous single-PR closed forms per cell x {P0, P1}: with p(w) = 1 - e^(-lambda*w) and per-policy windows (w1, w2): P(N>=1) = p(w1); P(N>=2) = p(w1)*p(w2); E[N] = p(w1)*e^(lambda*w2); P0: w1 = W+V, w2 = d+V; P1: w1 = w2 = V
arm_a.self_check: each quantity computed twice by independent float paths (1-exp vs -expm1; p(w1)*exp(lambda*w2) vs p(w1)/(1-p(w2))) agreeing within 1e-9 relative

validation_gate.exogenous_agreement: |ArmS_exogenous - ArmA| <= 1.0 percentage point ABSOLUTE on P(N>=1) and P(N>=2) in every covered cell (9 cells x {P0, P1} = 36 comparisons)
validation_gate.p3_zero_collisions: every endogenous P3 leg (primary, stability, all sensitivity legs, jitter) must report exactly ZERO collisions
validation_gate.on_failure: the run is INVALID — a failed gate is a sim bug by construction; debug, never ship

band_constants.comparison: exact Fraction arithmetic on pooled integer counts (renumbers, treadmill count, merged count) in the decision path — never floats; the twin evaluator uses integer cross-multiplication (10*renumbers <= merged; 100*tread2 <= merged; 20*tread2 > merged)

decision_rule.population: the ENDOGENOUS P1 cells of the primary leg (9 cells)
decision_rule.APPROVE: 'Option 1 suffices fleet-wide — retire Options 2/3 with the quantified reason' iff R <= 0.10 AND T <= 0.01 in >= 8 of the 9 cells, with the validation gate passed; checked FIRST — the build-nothing arm, so an eager build recommendation cannot shadow it (the PROPOSAL 019 evaluation-order discipline)
decision_rule.REJECT: 'the shared append point must go — route the Option-3 assign-at-merge build' iff T > 0.05 in >= 5 of the 9 cells; the verdict must attach the per-cell residual-tax table {R, T, latency inflation vs P3} as the Option-3 plan's evidence row
decision_rule.NULL: otherwise — a legitimate and here EXPECTED-USEFUL outcome: the verdict names the flip axis (for each of lambda and V, the APPROVE-pass share and median T at each of its values; largest spread = the named boundary — the idea file's expected candidate: V, because the checker cannot shrink the push->merge window and lambda*V is the collision pressure per round), and the citable finding is the CONDITIONAL per-PR-class rule
decision_rule.median: median over the 3 cells at each axis value; odd count so the middle value (exact Fraction)

reporting_only_legs.note: reporting-only — none can flip the decision (pre-registered)

stability_leg.requirement: must reproduce the same ruling as the primary leg (done-when)

jitter_leg.definition: W, V, d redrawn exponentially at the pinned means (tests fixed-window knife-edging): W once per PR at open, V at the start of every validation round, d at every fix

reporting_only_legs.anchor_leg: #1279 anchor — under (lambda = 12/day, V = 2 h, d = 0.5 h, P0): report Arm A's exact E[N], P(N>=3), P(N>=4) plus the endogenous measured counterparts from the primary leg at that cell; a plausibility report against an n=1 anecdote (four renumbers in one afternoon), NEVER a fit

intake_time_decisions.note: committed HERE before the runner was written; each is reporting-only or implementation-level — the model, grids, seeds, M for the decision and gate legs, bands, and the decision rule are untouched
intake_time_decisions.1_sens_and_jitter_M: the idea file pins M=40 (primary), M=20,000 (exogenous), M=8 (stability) but leaves M for the d/W sensitivity legs and the jitter leg unpinned; pinned at M=8, mirroring the stability leg's committed scale (the VERDICT 021 precedent); each sensitivity leg runs on a fresh random.Random(20260723) stream, the jitter leg on its own pinned random.Random(20260725)
intake_time_decisions.2_jitter_draw_semantics: the idea file says 'redrawing W, V, d exponentially with the pinned means' without pinning the redraw granularity; pinned: W once per PR, V per validation round, d per fix, via rng.expovariate(1/mean); draw order: arrival gaps for the whole replication first, then event-time draws in event order — at open (W, V_round1), at each collision (d, V_next); draw-count identity: jitter draws per rep = arrivals(W) + arrivals + collisions(V) + collisions(d)
intake_time_decisions.3_exogenous_truncation: each exogenous focal PR is simulated only through its first TWO merge-attempt rounds (N recorded as 0, 1, or >=2) — sufficient for the gate metrics P(N>=1) and P(N>=2), which are all the gate reads; necessitated by the closed form's own arithmetic (E[N] = p(w1)*e^(lambda*w2) ~ 2.1e5 rounds per focal PR at the harshest cell — infeasible and irrelevant to the gate); E[N] is Arm A's exact output, and R_exo in the amplification ratio = Arm A's E[N] (the idea file's own 'measured against the exact exogenous closed form')
intake_time_decisions.4_warmup_population: 'merged-PR population after warm-up' pinned as merge instant strictly > 200 h, full per-PR attributes (see process.warmup_population)
intake_time_decisions.5_p95_convention: index ceil(0.95*n) - 1 on the sorted pooled durations
intake_time_decisions.6_inflation_convention: latency inflation vs P3 = mean-duration ratio and p95-duration ratio against the same cell's measured P3 leg (same leg, same seed stream) — reporting-only floats
intake_time_decisions.7_amplification_scope: R_endo/R_exo reported per cell for BOTH P0 and P1 (the decision reads neither; the deliverable is the P1 row)

consequence_preregistered.APPROVE: the canonical doc's remaining Options 2/3 are retired with the quantified reason (routed lane-side via the manager sweep, Q-0260 — the doc is superbot's, never edited from here); the only remaining slice is the doc's own unfinished ask, wiring the shipped checker into the Stop hook / pre-pr step
consequence_preregistered.REJECT: the Option-3 assign-at-merge design (the doc's 'own plan') gets its evidence row: the measured residual treadmill UNDER the shipped guard, per cell, plus the latency tax — the priced difference between 'we have a checker' and 'the race is gone'
consequence_preregistered.NULL: the conditional per-PR-class rule ships, and the cheapest LIVE probe is named: superbot's REAL (lambda, V, W, d) from one git log pass over migrations/ merge timestamps and PR open/push/merge times, locating the repo's actual cell before anyone spends an infra build on a corner of the grid it does not occupy

boundaries_preregistered.poisson_arrivals: real fleet merges bunch; the endogenous mode generates bunching from the mechanism itself and the jitter leg redraws all windows, but exogenous burst processes are out of scope by design
boundaries_preregistered.uniform_compliance: every simulated author runs the checker on every push under P1; real compliance is <= that, so P1's simulated residual is a FLOOR — an APPROVE is the fragile direction, a REJECT is conservative
boundaries_preregistered.one_repo_one_sequence: cross-repo or per-directory sequences are not modeled
boundaries_preregistered.models_are_models: lambda, V, W, d are pinned dials, not measurements — the NULL consequence names the git-history probe that locates superbot's real cell

HAND-1.derivation: fully hand-derived before code ran (P1 picks at push). PR0 opens t=0, picks at push t=8: max=0 -> holds 1. PR1 opens t=1, picks at push t=9: max still 0 -> ALSO holds 1 (simultaneous holders of number 1 = 2, the race made visible). PR0 attempts t=10: held 1 > max 0 -> MERGE, max=1, duration 10, N=0. PR1 attempts t=11: held 1 <= max 1 -> COLLISION (N=1; PR0's merge at t=10 sits inside PR1's exposure window (9, 11] of length V=2 exactly); fix until 11.5, re-pick at push 11.5: max=1 -> holds 2; attempts 13.5: 2 > 1 -> MERGE, max=2, duration 12.5, N=1. Totals: merged 2, renumbers 1, treadmill(>=2) 0, collisions 1, max_main 2, max simultaneous holders 2, durations 10 and 13.5-1 = 12.5, duration sum 22.5, R = 1/2, T = 0. (Amended after the runner existed and its twin implementations both computed 22.5: the originally committed expectation carried a hand-arithmetic slip, 10 + 12.5 written as 23.5; every OTHER expected value stood as derived, and no decision constant was touched.)

HAND-2.derivation: fully hand-derived (P0 picks at open and at each fix start; a three-way pile shows the chained treadmill). Picks at open: PR0 t=0 holds 1, PR1 t=1 holds 1, PR2 t=2 holds 1 (simultaneous holders of number 1 = 3). PR0 attempts t=10 (window W+V=10): merge, max=1, dur 10, N=0. PR1 attempts t=11: held 1 <= 1 -> COLLISION (N=1), picks AT FIX START t=11: max=1 -> holds 2; attempts t=11+0.5+2=13.5: 2 > 1 -> merge, max=2, dur 12.5, N=1. PR2 attempts t=12: held 1 <= 2 -> COLLISION (N=1), picks t=12: max=1 -> holds 2 (PR1 still holds 2 until 13.5: simultaneous holders of 2 = 2); attempts t=14.5: held 2 <= max 2 (PR1 merged 2 at 13.5, inside PR2's round-2 window (12, 14.5] of length d+V=2.5 exactly) -> COLLISION (N=2), picks t=14.5: holds 3; attempts t=17: 3 > 2 -> merge, max=3, dur 15, N=2. Totals at warmup=0: merged 3, renumbers 3, treadmill 1 (PR2), collisions 3, max_main 3, max_holders 3, dur sum 37.5, R = 1, T = 1/3. Re-scored at warmup=10.5 (tests the merge-instant filter): merged = {PR1@13.5, PR2@17} = 2, renumbers 3, treadmill 1, dur sum 27.5, R = 3/2, T = 1/2.

seed_registry_note: seeds 20260723-26 fresh per the idea file (the registry swept across prior heads 20260713-22 before picking; the PROPOSAL 020 card's collision warning heeded)
